quotes.quote: Everything in IT — I mean everything — runs on a network. Cloud? Network. Cybersecurity? Network. DevOps? Still a network underneath. You cannot skip this foundation.
quotes.context: Opening of his free CCNA series
quotes.why_they_said_it: He gets questions constantly asking if networking is still relevant in the cloud era.

quotes.quote: A subnet mask is just telling your device: this part of the IP address is the street you live on, and this part is your house number. The subnet mask is the dividing line.
quotes.why_they_said_it: Most people get lost in the binary math before understanding what subnetting is FOR.

quotes.quote: Linux is not scary. I promise. It is literally just a different language. And once you start to understand it, Windows starts to feel... limited.
quotes.why_they_said_it: His audience is intimidated. He was too.

quotes.quote: You do not need expensive equipment. My first home lab was $40 of used Cisco gear from eBay and a $35 Raspberry Pi. That was enough to pass my CCNA.
quotes.why_they_said_it: He regularly gets told 'I can't afford to learn this.'

quotes.quote: Stop waiting until you feel ready. You will never feel ready. Set up the lab. Break something. Fix it. That is literally the entire job described.
quotes.context: How to break into IT video
quotes.why_they_said_it: Analysis paralysis kills IT career transitions.

quotes.quote: The moment I realised I could configure 50 switches in 2 minutes with a Python script instead of logging into each one manually — I became obsessed.
quotes.why_they_said_it: He teaches Python through network automation because that is his actual experience.
